member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext
member.summary: Holds the active data isolation and permission configuration for the MCP server session. Registered as a singleton; populated from McpServer appsettings on startup.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.Level
member.summary: Active isolation level. Defaults to MultiTenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.TenantIdentifier
member.summary: Tenant identifier used when Level is Tenant, Division, or Employee. All tool calls are automatically scoped to this tenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.DivisionName
member.summary: Division name used when Level is Division. All tool calls are automatically scoped to this division within the configured tenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.EmployeeIdentifier
member.summary: Employee identifier used when Level is Employee. All tool calls are automatically scoped to this employee within the configured tenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.PreviewUserIdentifier
member.summary: User identifier used as the service account for payrun preview and report execution. Required when the get_employee_pay_preview or execute_payroll_report tool is used. The user must exist in the target tenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.Permissions
member.summary: Role permission levels for this deployment. Defaults to Full for all roles when absent from configuration.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext.Validate
member.summary: Validates the isolation context at startup. Throws if configuration is incomplete or uses an invalid combination.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel
member.summary: Data isolation level for the MCP server. Controls which tenants, divisions, and employees are accessible within a session.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel.MultiTenant
member.summary: Full access across all tenants. No automatic scoping is applied.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel.Tenant
member.summary: Access restricted to a single tenant configured at startup via McpServer:TenantIdentifier.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel.Division
member.summary: Access restricted to a single division within a tenant. Requires McpServer:TenantIdentifier and McpServer:DivisionName.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel.Employee
member.summary: Access restricted to a single employee — self-service scenarios. Requires McpServer:TenantIdentifier and McpServer:EmployeeIdentifier.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermission
member.summary: Access level granted for a role in this MCP Server deployment. Ordered: None < Read < Write — use >= comparisons for minimum permission checks.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermission.None
member.summary: Role tools are not registered — invisible to the AI agent.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermission.Read
member.summary: Read and query tools only.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermission.Write
member.summary: Read and write tools. Includes all Read tools plus mutation operations (case changes, payrun execution, employee mutations). Available in PayrollEngine.Mcp.Server.Pro only.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions
member.summary: Permission levels per role for this MCP Server deployment. Populated from McpServer:Permissions in appsettings.json or environment variables. Default when the Permissions section is absent: Read for all roles.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.HR
member.summary: Permission for HR tools (divisions, employees, case values).

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.Payroll
member.summary: Permission for Payroll tools (payrolls, payruns, jobs, temporal queries).

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.Report
member.summary: Permission for Report tools (payroll report execution).

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.System
member.summary: Permission for System tools (tenant and user queries).

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.GetPermission(PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole)
member.summary: Returns the permission granted for the given role.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.IsGranted(PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole,PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermission)
member.summary: Returns true if the given role is granted at least the required permission.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions.FromConfiguration(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration)
member.summary: Reads McpServer:Permissions from configuration. Returns a fully-read instance when the section is absent.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole
member.summary: Functional domain to which a tool class belongs. Each tool class is assigned to exactly one role.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole.HR
member.summary: Employee master data and organisational structure: divisions, employees, case values.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole.Payroll
member.summary: Payroll execution and result verification: payrolls, payruns, payrun jobs, temporal case value queries.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole.Report
member.summary: Payroll report execution and result analysis.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole.System
member.summary: Tenant and user management.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute
member.summary: Declares the role and minimum permission required to register a tool class. Applied to classes decorated with [McpServerToolType]. Tool classes without this attribute are always registered regardless of permissions.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute.Role
member.summary: The functional domain this tool class belongs to.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute.Required
member.summary: Minimum permission level required to register this tool class. Defaults to Read.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole,PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermission)
member.param: The role this tool class belongs to.
Minimum permission to register. Default: Read.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase
member.summary: Base class for all MCP tool classes. Provides typed service factories, identifier-to-id resolvers, and uniform error handling. AI agents work exclusively with external string keys (Identifier/Name); internal integer IDs are resolved here before calling id-based service methods.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: Base class for all MCP tool classes. Provides typed service factories, identifier-to-id resolvers, and uniform error handling. AI agents work exclusively with external string keys (Identifier/Name); internal integer IDs are resolved here before calling id-based service methods.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.HttpClient
member.summary: The Payroll HTTP client used for backend communication

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.Isolation
member.summary: The active data isolation context. Every tool call is filtered through this context.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ActiveQuery(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String)
member.summary: Builds a Query with status=Active filter, optionally combined with an additional OData filter. All list operations use this to exclude inactive objects by default.
member.param: Optional additional OData filter from the caller

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ActiveDivisionQuery(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Builds a DivisionQuery with status=Active filter. Required for EmployeeService.QueryAsync which expects DivisionQuery.
member.param: Optional additional OData filter from the caller

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.IsolatedTenantQuery
member.summary: Builds a tenant-scoped active query. In any non-MultiTenant isolation mode, adds an identifier filter so list_tenants only returns the configured tenant.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.IsolatedEmployeeQuery(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Builds an employee-scoped active DivisionQuery. In Employee isolation mode, adds an identifier filter so list_employees only returns the configured employee. In Division isolation mode, no OData division filter is applied because the backend does not support collection lambda expressions (divisions/any()). Division filtering is applied client-side via after the query returns.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.FilterEmployeesByIsolation(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{PayrollEngine.Client.Model.Employee})
member.summary: Filters a list of employees client-side to enforce Division isolation. In Division mode, only employees that belong to the configured division are returned. In Employee mode, only the configured employee is returned. In all other modes the list is returned unchanged.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.AssertEmployeeInDivision(PayrollEngine.Client.Model.Employee)
member.summary: Validates that an already-resolved employee belongs to the configured division. In Division isolation mode, throws if the employee is not a member of the configured division. In other isolation modes this is a no-op.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.IsolatedDivisionQueryAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Builds a division-scoped active query, including Employee isolation support. In Division isolation mode, adds a name filter so list_divisions returns only the configured division. In Employee isolation mode, adds a filter limited to the employee's own division(s).

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.IsolatedPayrollQueryAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Builds an active query for payrolls filtered by the isolated division. In Division isolation mode, returns only payrolls belonging to the configured division.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.IsolatedPayrunQueryAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Builds an active query for payruns filtered by the isolated division. In Division isolation mode, returns only payruns whose payroll belongs to the configured division.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.Error(System.Exception)
member.summary: Serializes a caught exception as a structured JSON error result. All public tool methods must catch exceptions and delegate to this method so the MCP client always receives valid JSON, never a raw exception message.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.TenantService
member.summary: Creates a new tenant service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.UserService
member.summary: Creates a new user service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.DivisionService
member.summary: Creates a new division service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.EmployeeService
member.summary: Creates a new employee service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.RegulationService
member.summary: Creates a new regulation service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.WageTypeService
member.summary: Creates a new wage type service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.LookupService
member.summary: Creates a new lookup service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.LookupValueService
member.summary: Creates a new lookup value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.PayrollService
member.summary: Creates a new payroll service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.PayrunService
member.summary: Creates a new payrun service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.PayrunJobService
member.summary: Creates a new payrun job service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.PayrollResultService
member.summary: Creates a new payroll result service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.EmployeeCaseValueService
member.summary: Creates a new employee case value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.CompanyCaseValueService
member.summary: Creates a new company case value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.EmployeeCaseChangeService
member.summary: Creates a new employee case change service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.CompanyCaseChangeService
member.summary: Creates a new company case change service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.PayrollResultValueService
member.summary: Creates a new payroll result value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ReportService
member.summary: Creates a new report service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.PayrollConsolidatedResultService
member.summary: Creates a new payroll consolidated result service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.EffectiveTenant(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the effective tenant identifier, enforcing isolation. In Tenant, Division, and Employee modes the configured identifier overrides whatever the AI agent passed.
member.param: Identifier provided by the AI agent

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.EffectiveEmployee(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the effective employee identifier, enforcing Employee isolation. In Employee mode the configured identifier overrides whatever the AI agent passed.
member.param: Identifier provided by the AI agent

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveIsolatedDivisionIdAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves the division id for the active isolation context. In Division mode returns the id of the configured division; otherwise null.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveTenantAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a tenant by its identifier, applying isolation enforcement. In Tenant mode, the configured tenant identifier always takes precedence.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveTenantContextAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a TenantServiceContext by tenant identifier

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveUserAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a user by tenant identifier and user identifier

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveDivisionAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a division by tenant identifier and division name

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveEmployeeAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves an employee by tenant identifier and employee identifier, applying isolation enforcement. In Employee mode the configured employee identifier always takes precedence.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveEmployeeContextAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves an EmployeeServiceContext by tenant and employee identifier. Required for EmployeeCaseValueService which needs EmployeeServiceContext.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveRegulationContextAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a RegulationServiceContext by tenant identifier and regulation name

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveLookupContextAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a LookupServiceContext by tenant, regulation and lookup name. Required for LookupValueService which needs LookupServiceContext.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolvePayrollContextAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a PayrollServiceContext by tenant identifier and payroll name

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolBase.ResolveReportAsync(PayrollEngine.Client.Service.PayrollServiceContext,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a ReportSet by payroll context and report name. Returns the topmost derived report across all regulation layers.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolRegistrar
member.summary: Filters and registers MCP tool classes based on the active McpPermissions and the active IsolationLevel. Tool classes tagged with [ToolRole] are only registered when: (a) the deployment grants at least the required permission for that role, AND (b) the role is applicable for the active isolation level (per Role x IsolationLevel matrix). Tool classes without [ToolRole] are always registered.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolRegistrar.GetPermittedTypes(System.Reflection.Assembly,PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpPermissions,PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel)
member.summary: Returns all tool types from the given assembly that pass the permission and isolation-level compatibility filter.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.ToolRegistrar.IsRoleCompatibleWithIsolationLevel(PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.McpRole,PayrollEngine.Mcp.Core.Isolation.IsolationLevel)
member.summary: Returns true when the given role is applicable for the given isolation level. Based on the Role x IsolationLevel matrix defined in README.md: HR: all levels; Payroll: all except Employee; Report and System: MultiTenant and Tenant only.
